acceptance_targets: [CH07_DIALOGUE_01_CALIBRATION_ECHO] Pass if the revised exchange at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:115-119 no longer uses the recognizable wording or sequence of the style-bible example line while still making legible that Kai's mother responds to his pre-enhancement self as distinct from his current body. Fail if a cold reader could recognize the calibration example on first read.

[CH07_DIALOGUE_02_COMPOSED_REGISTER] Pass if the revised dialogue at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:73-93, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:101-111, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:169-177, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:213-231 still conveys the same plot information but no longer contains standalone thesis or aphoristic lines equivalent to 'My body was the program's body,' 'The gaps swim better than I do,' or 'The gap has a shape,' and each exchange includes at least one observable roughness such as a self-correction, interruption, partial answer, or sideways reply. Fail if the scene can still be skimmed as a sequence of polished summaries.

[CH07_CRAFT_01_OVERCERTIFIED_MEANING] Pass if the revised passages at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:97-99, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:187, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:229, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:249-255 no longer function mainly as explanations of emotions or meanings the reader already has, and instead either create a new observable consequence or are cut. Fail if those passages still primarily paraphrase the significance of the preceding line, gesture, or silence.

[CH07_PROSE_01_NEGATION_TIC] Pass if, in the revised versions of snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:3, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:13, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:77, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:169, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:229, no more than two of the cited beats still rely on a negation-led definition and the others name the sensation or shift directly. Fail if the chapter continues to stack the same exclusion-first construction across these pressure points.

[STYLE_ECHO_CH07] Lines 1241-1245 contain no phrase matching 'she says she can tell the difference' or 'the natural stuff' from the style-bible example. The mother's observation is conveyed in language original to the manuscript.

[PROSE_WRIST_ACHE_chapter_07] Line 1129 paragraph is 3-4 sentences. The healing-speed wrongness is conveyed through one concrete sensory detail and one thematic beat, not through explanatory walkthrough.
[PROSE_PORT_TOUCH_chapter_07] Line 1195 does not use the phrase 'small disc.' Kai's observation of the port is framed through his POV, not through inventory.

[READER_RETEACH_chapter_07] Line 1133 describes the hand experiments through action and sensation without re-explaining predictive motor drift. Lines 1209-1219 reference the baseline gap in dialogue shorthand, not as a narrator's explanatory summary.
[R004] Ch 7 does not contain the phrase 'hands were steady' or 'engineer's hands' in reference to Yusuf.
[R012] Ch 7 contains no more than one instance of the word 'institutional.'
[R016] Ch 7 contains no more than one instance of 'fluorescent.'
[R047] Ch 7 contains no more than 5 instances of 'something.'
[C003] Ch 7 contains no double-hyphen sequences (' -- '); all dashes in Ch 7 are em-dashes (—) matching the other 17 chapters.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised exchange at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:115-119 no longer uses the recognizable wording or sequence of the style-bible example line while still making legible that Kai's mother responds to his pre-enhancement self as distinct from his current body. Fail if a cold reader could recognize the calibration example on first read.
findings.problem: Kai's disclosure about his mother watching the old races is a conspicuous near-echo of a style-bible calibration-only example line. Because it lands inside one of the chapter's central intimacy beats, the reuse reads as lifted calibration language rather than fresh character speech and damages voice integrity on first read.
findings.rewrite_direction: Replace the dialogue centered on snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:115-119 with new material that keeps the same dramatic function but finds a different memory, syntax, and entry point. Preserve the facts that Kai's mother prefers the pre-enhancement version of him and that he can supply a biomechanical difference, but do not reuse the 'old footage / she can tell the difference' phrasing or its beat order.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised dialogue at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:73-93, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:101-111, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:169-177, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:213-231 still conveys the same plot information but no longer contains standalone thesis or aphoristic lines equivalent to 'My body was the program's body,' 'The gaps swim better than I do,' or 'The gap has a shape,' and each exchange includes at least one observable roughness such as a self-correction, interruption, partial answer, or sideways reply. Fail if the scene can still be skimmed as a sequence of polished summaries.
findings.problem: Across the dinner and walk scenes, the dominant dialogue register is composed rather than lived. The chapter leans on thesis-speech ('My body was the program's body'), aphoristic or quotable lines ('The gaps swim better than I do'; 'The gap has a shape'), and mini-speech diagnosis in places where Kai and Lena should sound more provisional, pressured, or sideways. There are no mic-drop rhetorical questions here, but the chapter still overuses polished summaries and clean thematic landings, so the scene reads written-about rather than spoken-through.
findings.rewrite_direction: Roughen the exchanges at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:73-93, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:101-111, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:169-177, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:213-231. Keep the factual beats (Lena has no baseline, their homesickness is asymmetrical, the desync can outperform Lena, Kai is testing his hand for autonomous timing), but break the clean thesis lines into more character-true speech: partial answers, concrete detours, self-corrections, interruptions, or responses that land on the wrong part of the question before circling back.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if the revised passages at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:97-99, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:187, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:229, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:249-255 no longer function mainly as explanations of emotions or meanings the reader already has, and instead either create a new observable consequence or are cut. Fail if those passages still primarily paraphrase the significance of the preceding line, gesture, or silence.
findings.problem: Once an exchange or image has already landed, the chapter often certifies its meaning in abstract narration instead of letting the new relationship state live in behavior. The repeated glossing at lines 97, 187, 229, and 249 turns a quiet chapter that should feel inhabited into one that keeps explaining its own tenderness and interpretive stakes.
findings.rewrite_direction: Trim or repurpose the explanatory aftermath at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:97-99, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:187, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:229, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:249-255. Each cited beat should either add a new observable consequence (movement, touch, withdrawal, silence with cost, changed choice) or disappear. In particular, let the chapter ending at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:245-255 close on altered behavior or image rather than narratorial paraphrase of what the pause means.

findings.acceptance_test: Pass if, in the revised versions of snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:3, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:13, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:77, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:169, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:229, no more than two of the cited beats still rely on a negation-led definition and the others name the sensation or shift directly. Fail if the chapter continues to stack the same exclusion-first construction across these pressure points.
findings.problem: A marked negative-definition scaffold recurs across the chapter's key perception beats: the prose keeps defining sensation or understanding by first listing what it is not and only then naming what it is. Used once or twice, that is control. Used this often, it becomes chapter-level autopilot and blunts the bodily immediacy the style bible asks for.
findings.rewrite_direction: Recast the sentences at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:3, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:13, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:77, snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:169, and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_07.md:229 so most of those beats are rendered positively or through direct sensory/action detail rather than through 'not X / not Y / but Z' exclusion. Keep at most one or two negation-driven landings if they are doing unique work; do not let the same sentence shape carry the chapter's major realizations.

findings.acceptance_test: Lines 1241-1245 contain no phrase matching 'she says she can tell the difference' or 'the natural stuff' from the style-bible example. The mother's observation is conveyed in language original to the manuscript.

evidence.excerpt: 1245: "She says she can tell the difference. She says I used to lean differently out of the blocks. Which is true -- the grafts changed my push-off angle by about three degrees. But I don't think she's analyzing biomechanics. I think she's looking at her son versus whatever she thinks I am now."

rewrite_direction.excerpt:
1241: "My mom still watches my old race footage," he said. "The pre-enhancement stuff. She records herself reacting to it and sends me the videos. It's a lot."
1242: 
1243: "Does she like the old races better?"
1244: 
1245: "She says she can tell the difference. She says I used to lean differently out of the blocks. Which is true -- the grafts changed my push-off angle by about three degrees. But I don't think she's analyzing biomechanics. I think she's looking at her son versus whatever she thinks I am now."

findings.problem: Near-verbatim echo of style-bible calibration example line. Kai says 'She says she can tell the difference' about his mother watching old race footage. The style bible's calibration-only example line for Kai reads: 'My mom still watches the old footage. The natural stuff. She says she can tell the difference.' The manuscript version at line 1241-1245 closely tracks this: 'My mom still watches my old race footage. The pre-enhancement stuff... She says she can tell the difference.' The phrasing 'she says she can tell the difference' is identical.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:1241-1245, rephrase the mother's observation to avoid matching the calibration example. The story beat (mother notices a difference in old vs. new footage) is earned and should stay. Change the specific phrasing: e.g., 'She says something changed in how I move' or 'She told me last week she can see it — the difference between me and whatever I am now.' The point is the same; the words must diverge from the example.

findings.acceptance_test: Line 1129 paragraph is 3-4 sentences. The healing-speed wrongness is conveyed through one concrete sensory detail and one thematic beat, not through explanatory walkthrough.

evidence.excerpt: 1129: The brace was off. Petra had cleared him to remove it for short periods, and the bone had knitted -- faster than any break had a right to mend. Not healed, exactly. Knitting implied the body's own patient labor, tissue finding tissue on its own schedule. This was different. The synthetic grafts had driven the bone repair forward on a timeline that had nothing to do with his pain tolerance or his patience, and the wrist had closed itself up six weeks ahead of the curve. The itch was deep inside, below the skin where the brace had rubbed raw, down in the bone itself where new tissue met old -- a wrongness that wasn't pain but speed, his body rebuilding on a schedule he didn't set.

rewrite_direction.excerpt: 1129: The brace was off. Petra had cleared him to remove it for short periods, and the bone had knitted -- faster than any break had a right to mend. Not healed, exactly. Knitting implied the body's own patient labor, tissue finding tissue on its own schedule. This was different. The synthetic grafts had driven the bone repair forward on a timeline that had nothing to do with his pain tolerance or his patience, and the wrist had closed itself up six weeks ahead of the curve. The itch was deep inside, below the skin where the brace had rubbed raw, down in the bone itself where new tissue met old -- a wrongness that wasn't pain but speed, his body rebuilding on a schedule he didn't set.

findings.problem: Extended establishing description of the wrist's too-fast healing. This is the first full post-injury treatment and earns its length, but sets the template that subsequent chapters copy.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:1129, retain as establishing passage but tighten: the final clause ('his body rebuilding on a schedule he didn't set') carries the thematic weight. Cut or compress the middle ('Knitting implied the body's own patient labor' is good but the surrounding sentences over-explain).

findings.acceptance_test: Line 1195 does not use the phrase 'small disc.' Kai's observation of the port is framed through his POV, not through inventory.

evidence.excerpt: 1195: "Sure." He looked at her across the institutional table. The fluorescent lighting did what fluorescent lighting always did -- washed the color out of everything, made the trays and the food and the faces all look like they existed on the same flat plane. He could see the neural link port at the base of her skull where her hair was pulled up. A small disc, almost but not quite the color of her skin. "Did you like it? The program?"

rewrite_direction.excerpt: 1195: "Sure." He looked at her across the institutional table. The fluorescent lighting did what fluorescent lighting always did -- washed the color out of everything, made the trays and the food and the faces all look like they existed on the same flat plane. He could see the neural link port at the base of her skull where her hair was pulled up. A small disc, almost but not quite the color of her skin. "Did you like it? The program?"

findings.problem: Kai observes the port: 'the neural link port at the base of her skull where her hair was pulled up. A small disc, almost but not quite the color of her skin.' Re-uses the 'small disc' construction from Ch 2.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:1195, since this is Kai's POV (not Lena's), the observation should reflect his perception. He wouldn't catalog the disc's dimensions — he'd notice it the way you notice a hearing aid or a surgical scar: as a fact about someone's body that you register without examining. Compress to something observational rather than descriptive.

findings.acceptance_test: Line 1133 describes the hand experiments through action and sensation without re-explaining predictive motor drift. Lines 1209-1219 reference the baseline gap in dialogue shorthand, not as a narrator's explanatory summary.

evidence.excerpt: 1133: The experiments had started by accident three days ago. He'd been reaching for a glass of water and caught the movement early -- his hand arriving at the glass a fraction ahead of his decision to reach. Normally he let these moments pass, the way you let your hand find a light switch in a dark room without thinking about how it got there. But this time he'd frozen with the glass in his fingers and tried to feel inside the gap. It was there -- not visible, not painful, just a tiny anticipatory firing in the rebuilt forearm muscle, the synthetic tissue queuing the motion before his conscious intent arrived. The system reading his mind and jumping the queue.

1209: "I had twenty-seven years of knowing what my body felt like before they rebuilt it. I've got a whole baseline sitting there. I know what running felt like when it was just me, because I spent a decade doing it. I can feel the gap." He turned his coffee cup on the table a quarter rotation, no reason. "You don't have that."

1219: "No, it's fine. You're right. I don't know what my baseline is. Maybe I've never had one." She set her fork down. "When I was small, five or six, I had calibration sessions twice a week. I'd lie in the rig and the technician would say, can you feel this, can you feel that. And I always said yes. But I don't know now if what I was feeling was the calibration or my own nervous system reacting to the calibration. I can't separate them. There isn't a way to untangle it."

rewrite_direction.excerpt: 1133: The experiments had started by accident three days ago. He'd been reaching for a glass of water and caught the movement early -- his hand arriving at the glass a fraction ahead of his decision to reach. Normally he let these moments pass, the way you let your hand find a light switch in a dark room without thinking about how it got there. But this time he'd frozen with the glass in his fingers and tried to feel inside the gap. It was there -- not visible, not painful, just a tiny anticipatory firing in the rebuilt forearm muscle, the synthetic tissue queuing the motion before his conscious intent arrived. The system reading his mind and jumping the queue.

findings.problem: Ch 7 re-explains the proprioception drift (line 1133: 'his hand arriving at the glass a fraction ahead of his decision to reach... the synthetic tissue queuing the motion before his conscious intent arrived') — this was already explained in full in Ch 1 (line 147-168). Ch 7 also re-explains Lena's lack of a baseline (line 1209-1219) which was established in Ch 2.
findings.rewrite_direction: At snapshots/cycle_01/FINAL_NOVEL.md:1133, compress the hand-override experiments to show what's new (he's learning to feel the gap and resist it) without re-explaining what the gap is. At lines 1209-1219, the Kai-Lena conversation can reference 'you don't have that' and 'you can't tell if it's new' without Kai re-stating the full explanation of what proprioception drift means.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch 7 does not contain the phrase 'hands were steady' or 'engineer's hands' in reference to Yusuf.
findings.evidence: Ch 7 line 859: 'His hands were steady, the way they always were — an engineer's hands, precise and unhesitating.'; Ch 3 line 395: 'His hands were steady on the interface — they always were, the one part of him that never registered what was happening as strange.'; Ch 1 line 107: 'hands moving with the unhesitating precision of someone who'd done this ten thousand times.'
findings.problem: Yusuf's hand steadiness described three times in seven chapters using near-identical vocabulary ('steady,' 'precise,' 'unhesitating,' 'engineer's hands'). Ch 7 line 859 is functionally indistinguishable from Ch 3 line 395. The later inversion at Ch 14 line 2964 ('They were not steady now') works better if the trait has not been hammered.
findings.rewrite_direction: In Ch 7, delete the 'His hands were steady' sentence entirely or replace with a contrasting detail about what is different in this crisis moment.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch 7 contains no more than one instance of the word 'institutional.'
findings.evidence: Ch 7 lines 1149, 1183, 1195, 1225, 1251: five uses of 'institutional' in one chapter — 'institutional persistence' (coffee), 'institutional coffee' (dialogue), 'institutional table' (description), 'institutional broccoli' (food), 'institutional stuff' (coffee again). Manuscript-wide: 'institutional' appears 24 times across 13 chapters.
findings.problem: The word 'institutional' appears five times in Ch 7, a chapter that is a quiet cafeteria conversation. The reader has already absorbed the facility's institutional character from Ch 1 and Ch 3. Five instances in one chapter turns atmosphere into a verbal tic. Manuscript-wide, 24 uses of 'institutional' exceed what this descriptor needs to do.
findings.rewrite_direction: Keep one 'institutional' reference in Ch 7 (the strongest atmospheric beat). Replace the other four with specific sensory detail that evokes institutional atmosphere without the word itself. Manuscript-wide, target 8-10 retained instances.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch 7 contains no more than one instance of 'fluorescent.'
findings.evidence: Ch 7 lines 1165, 1195, 1369: three 'fluorescent' instances. Also Ch 7 contains 5 'institutional' uses (see R012). Combined, the chapter deploys 8 institutional/fluorescent descriptors.
findings.problem: Three fluorescent references in Ch 7, stacked on top of five 'institutional' uses. The cafeteria scene is over-described with facility-atmosphere signifiers.
findings.rewrite_direction: Keep one fluorescent reference in Ch 7. Combined with the R012 institutional reduction, the chapter's atmosphere should be conveyed through character behavior and dialogue rather than repeated setting adjectives.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch 7 contains no more than 5 instances of 'something.'
findings.evidence: Ch 7: 'something' appears 12 times in 4186 words = 1 per 349 words. Manuscript-wide: 190 occurrences in 78,027 words = 1 per 411 words (threshold: 1 per 800).
findings.problem: 'Something' appears 12 times in Ch 7, a rate of 1 per 349 words. This vague pronoun substitutes for specific imagery. Manuscript-wide target: reduce from 190 to ~98 occurrences.
findings.rewrite_direction: Reduce 'something' in Ch 7 from 12 to ~5 (7 cuts). Replace each cut with the specific noun, sensation, or concept the 'something' stands in for. Keep 'something' only where vagueness is the point (character genuinely cannot name the experience).

findings.acceptance_test: Ch 7 contains no double-hyphen sequences (' -- '); all dashes in Ch 7 are em-dashes (—) matching the other 17 chapters.
findings.evidence: Ch 7 (lines 1127-1383) uses double-hyphens with spaces (' -- ') in 35 instances as em-dash substitutes. All other 17 chapters use proper em-dashes (—). Example: Ch 7 line 1129: 'The bone had knitted -- faster than any break had a right to mend.'
findings.problem: Ch 7 is the only chapter using double-hyphens instead of em-dashes. This creates a visible formatting inconsistency when the manuscript is read as a compiled document. All 35 instances are in Ch 7; no other chapter exhibits this pattern.
findings.rewrite_direction: Convert all 35 double-hyphen instances in Ch 7 from ' -- ' to '—' (em-dash) to match the rest of the manuscript.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel asks what it means to own a body that has been partially replaced, optimized, or networked. The answer cannot be abstract. The physical horror of enhancement failure — sensory blackouts, autonomous muscle firing, personality thinning — must be rendered with visceral specificity. The intimacy between enhanced people must confront the fact that their bodies are partly engineered, and the strangeness of that must be present on the page, not elided. Margot's addiction to the sensory feed must be shown as a genuine addiction with genuine physical craving, not as an interesting character trait. Tomas's paralysis must be ugly, specific, and permanent — not a tidy plot device. The corporate betrayal must feel institutional, not melodramatic. When the novel asks its readers to sit with discomfort — a body that moves without consent, a mind that thins to nothing, an addiction that destroys someone else — the prose must stay present and not retreat to safety. The specific is always braver than the general.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism at the point of consequence is a craft failure. When a body crashes into a wall at sprint speed, the reader should feel the impact — bone, skin, blood, the specific geometry of injury. When Margot feels Tomas's spinal impact through the feed, the sensation must be rendered in full: the compression, the silence of nerves going dead, the phantom that stays after she disconnects. When Kai's body moves without his consent during sex, the reader must experience the uncanny specificity of reflexes that are not his. When Lena loses time, the gap must feel real — not a graceful ellipsis but a genuine absence. Every scene where the novel touches difficult material — bodily horror, addiction, institutional betrayal, the vulnerability of intimacy — must be rendered with the same specificity given to any other moment. Retreat into summary, metaphor, or tasteful vagueness at these moments destroys the novel's central claim that bodies matter.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters in spoken English. Uncontracted forms are reserved for emphasis, deliberate formality, or character-specific moments (Lena's slightly formal second-language register, Petra's clinical precision). Any uncontracted line in casual dialogue should be checked: does this character have a reason to be formal here?

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate high-intensity chapters with reflective or quiet ones. Vary scene count, dialogue density, and narrative distance across consecutive chapters. A chapter driven by confrontation should not be followed by another confrontation. A chapter heavy in dialogue should be followed by one that relies more on interiority and physical description. The quiet chapters (7, 16, parts of 18) should not feel like connective tissue between the 'real' scenes — they carry the human baseline that makes crisis meaningful. Some chapters should be physically compressed (a single long scene) and others structurally looser (multiple shorter scenes with white space between them).
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary literary — technically precise when inhabiting engineering or medical perspectives, grounded in body experience when inhabiting athletic or intimate perspectives. The enhancement technology should be described through felt sensation, not through exposition. How a neural link feels, not how it works. The novel's primary sensory vocabulary is the body: muscle, bone, nerve, heat, pressure, the experience of a body doing things its owner did not command.
forbidden_drift_patterns: Purple proprioception — the sensory descriptions of enhancement should remain specific and physical, not drift into poetic abstraction about the nature of consciousness
Exposition dumps about how the technology works — establish through character experience, not through narrator explanation
Uniform crisis register — not every chapter should feel urgent; some should feel ordinary, empty, or quiet, and the prose should match
Philosophical narrator — the narration should not editorialize about the body-autonomy theme; the events carry the argument
Symmetrical emotional beats — not every character should have matching epiphanies or matching moments of clarity; some characters end muddled
Mechanical metaphors for human experience — the point is that these people are not machines, even when their bodies partly are; do not let the prose treat them as systems
Identical blackout descriptions — each sensory disruption scene must find different language for the experience rather than reusing void/static/white metaphors
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, rotating POV by chapter. The narration inhabits each POV character's sensory and cognitive world — what they notice, how they process sensation, what language they would use internally. The narration should not be smarter, more articulate, or more philosophically aware than the character whose head we are in. Ensemble chapters may shift between characters within scenes but must establish clear transitions.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Variable across chapters and within chapters. Technical/calibration scenes: precise, measured, slightly clinical. Sensory/feed scenes: fractured, overlapping, synaesthetic. Interpersonal scenes: looser, more conversational, allowing silence and dead space. Action/blackout scenes: compressed, fragmented, physical. Quiet scenes: unhurried, sensory, present tense of ordinary experience. No single rhythm should dominate the novel.
